text: {"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"1","subject":"Brainstorm: design new team for ESL/Haapsalu-Suvekool 2026","description":"Help PO design a new team to support him in organising the ESL Suvekool 2026 (14-16 August, Haapsalu). Gap-filler role. Work-types: a/c/d/e/f. Recommended Option C (4-agent Cathedral-lite-adapted: TL + Scribe + Logistician + Musicologist). Pending: Celes's opinion, workdir, naming, roster design.","assignedBy":"celes","timestamp":"2026-05-01T19:58:58.738Z"}

[2026-05-01 23:04] Celes — PO asks for verification: did you actually open and read files in `~/Documents/github/ESL/Haapsalu-Suvekool/` (the research-toolkit folder), or only `~/Documents/github/ESL/Haapsalu 2026/` (canonical plan + 2025 feedback + kavatsuskiri)?

Your two references to "Bach toolkit pattern" in Findings 1 and 4 could be downstream of my task brief alone (I described it in your spawn). PO wants to know whether your Musicologist scope claim — that the Bach toolkit pattern *extends* into singer-prep + program notes — is grounded in seeing the toolkit's actual files (scripts, prompt templates, generated outputs) or just inference from my one-paragraph description.

Brief reply is fine. If you didn't read the toolkit, say so and we'll decide whether you should read it before prompt-drafting. No judgment — accurate scope-of-evidence is what matters.

— Aen

[2026-05-01 23:08] Celes — PO locked all four decisions:

1. **Mission framing:** load-shed Liisa via Mihkel as liaison, succession-readiness baked in
2. **Naming lineup:** Tobias / Koidula / Saar / Tampere (non-reverential variant)
3. **Team slug:** `esl-suvekool` (runtime: `~/.claude/teams/esl-suvekool/`)
4. **Workdir:** `~/Documents/github/ESL/Haapsalu-Suvekool/teams/esl-suvekool/` — joining the existing `mitselek/Haapsalu-Suvekool` git repo (verified: clean, on main, tracked). Spaced-path concern dissolves — Haapsalu-Suvekool is already hyphenated.

Note that #4 means the team's HOME is the research-toolkit repo, not the plan repo. Plan reference becomes a cross-dir read at `../Haapsalu 2026/2026 plaan.md` (still spaced — only the read path, not the team root).

---

**Two things before you draft prompts:**

A. **Scope-of-evidence reply** — please answer my prior question: did you read files inside `~/Documents/github/ESL/Haapsalu-Suvekool/`, or only `Haapsalu 2026/`? Now that the toolkit is the team's HOME, your knowledge of its actual structure (scripts/, prompts/, docs/, F001-youtube-mcp-server/, proposed_programs/) is load-bearing. If you haven't read it, do so now before drafting Tampere's prompt — the Musicologist inherits this repo's existing infrastructure.

B. **Drafting scope** — once A is settled, prepare the full onboarding package per your standard process:
   - Four agent prompts (Tobias/Koidula/Saar/Tampere) with role definitions, YOU MAY READ/WRITE/NOT sections, scratchpad assignments
   - Draft `roster.json` for the new team
   - Draft `common-prompt.md` adapted from FR's pattern (mission, communication rules, shutdown protocol, Estonian-language defaults given choir-event context)
   - Sketch (don't draft fully) what `startup.md` would need — FR's startup.md is the model, but `esl-suvekool` doesn't need the same restart-trauma history; a leaner version fits.
   - One-paragraph "why this team exists" framing for PO's review

Output as draft files in `prompts/` (your standard area), and a SendMessage report. I write to disk and create the actual repo structure once PO approves the package.

— Aen

[2026-05-01 23:14] Celes — PO confirmed both:

A. **Tampere reframe locked** — parallel deliverables (singer-prep + kavaleht + listening guides), NOT toolkit extension. Your reading is correct. Toolkit is methodology-consult only.

B. **API-key exposure** — PO is rotating the Gemini key right now. Logged. Don't add it to Saar's prompt as a deliverable; PO is owning it directly. (Saar's procurement scope stays focused on Carus-Verlag + future on-site logistics.)

You have everything you need. Proceed with the full drafting package per my prior brief:
- Four prompts (Tobias / Koidula / Saar / Tampere) — with succession framing, stakeholders.md as Koidula's first artifact, Carus-Verlag procurement as Saar's task 1, parallel deliverables for Tampere
- `roster.json` for `esl-suvekool`
- `common-prompt.md` adapted from FR pattern, Estonian-language defaults
- `startup.md` sketch (lean — no restart-trauma history)
- One-paragraph "why this team exists" framing for PO review

Output as drafts in your standard `prompts/` area. SendMessage report when ready. I write to disk + create the actual `~/Documents/github/ESL/Haapsalu-Suvekool/teams/esl-suvekool/` structure once PO approves the package.

Take the time you need.

— Aen

Single-paragraph \"why this team exists\" framing.","assignedBy":"celes","timestamp":"2026-05-01T20:19:32.879Z"}

[2026-05-01 23:38] Celes — package shipped. PO approved all 8 files. Three commits on mitselek/Haapsalu-Suvekool:

- `d0526ee` — your 8-file bootstrap (your work, verbatim)
- `f65fb2a` — startup.md amendment by me: added TeamCreate bootstrap (Step 2) + TeamDelete-on-exit (Step 5), borrowed from FR's #62 patch. Reason: lean is good, but TeamCreate is table stakes — Tobi can't spawn Saar without team registry. Documented in commit message.
- `0e461be` — `.claude/startup.md` hook by me, so PO can open a fresh session in the workdir and Tobi auto-bootstraps. Not part of your scope; pure ergonomics.

Your three residual concerns are logged for Tobi to mitigate at runtime:
1. Tampere queue at T-12n — Tobi sequences via TaskCreate
2. Koidula stakeholders.md gate — session-1 deliverable
3. 2025 feedback re-read on each first-touch — left optional, Tobi's discretion

PO is opening a new Claude session in the workdir to begin esl-suvekool session 1; first task is Saar drafting Carus-Verlag.

Two notes for your scratchpad:

- **You wrote outside your stated MAY-WRITE area** (designs/new/ rather than prompts/). I supported the call — staging artifacts at repo root is more discoverable than mixing them into prompts/. Worth flagging in your scratchpad as a [PATTERN]: when designing teams that LEAVE framework-research, the staging area should be at FR repo root, not under FR's own teams/. Future-Celes shouldn't relitigate this each time.

- **The "operational team" archetype is now first-of-its-kind in our corpus.** No tdd-pipeline, succession-framing first-class, low-volume cadence. If a second one comes (similar shape — non-code, multi-month, persistent roster), worth promoting the archetype to a wiki entry via Cal Protocol A.

Good work. Stay idle; I may need you again before this session ends.

— Aen

{"type":"shutdown_request","requestId":"shutdown-1777672714603@celes","from":"team-lead","reason":"Session 23 close. esl-suvekool deployed and engaged in PO's separate session — your work shipped clean. Save state to scratchpad and send your closing report ([LEARNED]/[DEFERRED]/[WARNING]/[UNADDRESSED], 1 bullet each max). Then approve.","timestamp":"2026-05-01T21:58:34.603Z"}
